disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Has missed the scheduled maintenance vitamin B12 (hydroxocobalamin) injections for about six weeks
trigger_question_examples: When did you last have your B12 injection?
How have you been going with your injections?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm about six weeks overdue — I just haven't been able to get out to have it done.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Constant, gradually worsening pins-and-needles and reduced sensation in both feet over recent weeks
trigger_question_examples: Can you tell me more about the tingling in your feet?
Has the numbness been changing or spreading?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: Both my feet have been tingling all the time and feel a bit numb, and it's been slowly getting worse the longer I've gone without the injection.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Profound whole-body fatigue with lightheadedness on movement and self-reported pallor
trigger_question_examples: How is your energy at the moment?
Do you feel dizzy or lightheaded?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: I'm completely wiped out, I get dizzy when I move around, and I've gone really pale.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Severe agoraphobia preventing the patient leaving home, driving repeated missed essential treatment
trigger_question_examples: What's been getting in the way of having your injections?
Is there something making it hard to get to the clinic?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: It's my anxiety — I get panicky about leaving the house, so I keep putting off going anywhere, even for my injections.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: Established need for the injectable (not oral) form because the patient cannot absorb B12 from food or tablets
trigger_question_examples: Do you know why you have the injections rather than tablets?
Have you ever taken B12 as a tablet instead?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: The doctors said tablets don't work for me because my body can't absorb it, so I have to have the injections.

disclosure_items.clinical_fact: No chest pain, no shortness of breath, no sudden or one-sided weakness, no loss of ability to move the legs
trigger_question_examples: Any chest pain or trouble breathing?
Any sudden weakness, or trouble moving your legs?
disclosure_items.patient_response_template: No chest pain or breathing trouble, and I can still move everything — it's just the tiredness and the tingling in my feet.
